my_thoughts: The denial/flexibility rule works: not altered, but flexibility rule (start from different point = not alteration), analogy (organic system: start from any point). The student asked authenticity check (checking teacher reliability), which shows they're checking teacher reliability. They'll likely ask about control next. Hypothesis: authenticity checks trigger power hierarchy questions—testable by tracking whether authenticity checks lead to power hierarchy inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student asks authenticity check ('Have you altered the teaching much?')—checking teacher reliability via asks for authenticity check and questions stability. They're checking teacher reliability (alteration). The structural_analogy mechanism denies: not altered, but flexibility rule (start from different point = not alteration), analogy (organic system: start from any point). Pattern: authenticity check → denial/flexibility rule → [next: control question]. Medium confidence because authenticity checks typically trigger clarification requests.
visible_response:
I have not altered it.
But I prefer sometimes to start from a different point.
In this
system, as in any organic system, one can start from any point—this is not an
alteration.
Everything else remains the same, but I prefer to start with the
psychological side.
